copyright_policy: Derived rule ids, phrase surfaces, counts, and calibration decisions only. No source prose is copied.

summary.main_finding: Several im-not-ai seed phrases are common in long-form webnovel prose. Treat ordinary grammar patterns as density warnings, while keeping explicit meta/summary/disclaimer patterns strict.

calibrations.reason: Common webnovel grammar in all supplied samples; simple occurrence blocking would produce false positives.
Still translationese-prone, but sample-backed enough to require density/context review before blocking.
Often appears as ordinary possession phrasing; flag clusters instead of isolated uses.
Very frequent in long samples; threshold must account for manuscript length until a normalized density auditor exists.
May appear in dialogue, in-world explanation, or source formatting. Multiple occurrences still require review.
Low sample frequency. Keep strict, but avoid blocking a single possible in-world sentence without context.
Still AI-summary-like in manuscripts; small threshold relaxation reduces isolated false positives.
Likely source-format artifact. Generated manuscript should still avoid Markdown bold markup.
Rare hit. Keep as a hard meta-AI guard unless a human confirms it is an intentional in-world term.

remaining_need: Add a length-normalized density auditor for AI-tell phrases.
Record short local evidence windows only in a private review file when the user explicitly permits source-prose inspection.
Separate source-format artifacts from manuscript-body artifacts before final guard review.
